Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Awarding Reasonable Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Dec-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS JEAN CHA , AN INDIVIDUAL, EDWIN SASAKI, and CHA LAW ETHIC's Motion For Order Awarding Reasonable Attorneys Fees.

Defendants Jean Cha, Cha Law Ethics and Edwin Sasaki's (defendants') motion for attorney fees is granted in the amount of $16,280. Defendants filed and won a CCP 425.16 anti-SLAPP motion against counts 6 (UCL) and 8 (unjust enrichment) of the complaint. As plaintiffs concede, "attorney's fees to a prevailing defendant on an Anti-SLAPP motion are mandatory." (Opp. 3:28-29.) Defendants won everything their motion sought and thus prevailed. Plaintiffs also concede defendants' challenges to the two counts "may not have technically been SLAPPbacks," after suggesting they were. (Opp. 3:6-26.) The $400 hourly rate is low by local standards, and defendants' briefing was well-written and persuasive.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliane with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
